commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=66b2f3bb8de1bc1a40947f269ce974aac0e67a55)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram default outbound text delivery from raw `sendRichMessage` to legacy `sendMessage` with HTML rendering and updates Telegram send/delivery regression tests.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +1. Total +51 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue has live multi-client unsupported-message evidence, and current main source still routes default Telegram text through `sendRichMessage`; I did not personally rerun the full live beta upgrade/downgrade path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the external PR still needs real Telegram proof and maintainer-visible resolution of the chunking compatibility blocker.

target.title: fix(telegram): default replies to legacy sendMessage
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #93263. This changes the default Telegram outbound reply path back to the widely supported Bot API `sendMessage` transport with `parse_mode: "HTML"`, instead of sending regular replies through Bot API 10.1 `sendRichMessage`. The regression in #93263 matches the rich-message path added in `2026.6.8-beta.1`: standard Telegram clients rendered every agent reply as "message is not supported". This PR keeps baseline Telegram delivery compatible while preserving legacy HTML rendering, chunking, reply/thread params, buttons, silent sends, and disabled link previews. ## Implementation - Change `sendMessageTelegram` default text sends to: - render Markdown/HTML into Telegram legacy HTML - send via `api.sendMessage(chatId, html, { parse_mode: "HTML" })` - chunk at the legacy 4096-character message limit - fall back to escaped plain HTML when Markdown chunk rendering produces no chunks - keep thread params, native reply params, inline keyboard on the last chunk, silent sends, and link-preview disabling - Change the delivery-layer final reply path (`sendTelegramText`) to the same legacy `sendMessage` transport. - Update Telegram send/delivery regression tests so ordinary final replies assert: - `sendMessage` is used - `sendRichMessage` is not used for default reply delivery - chunked messages stay bounded by 4096 chars - reply/thread/button/link-preview behavior remains intact ## Validation Local validation on a Linux checkout: - `corepack pnpm check:test-types` ✅ - `corepack pnpm test:extension telegram extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` ✅ - 2 test files passed - 182 tests passed - `git diff --check` ✅ ## Risk / rollback - Risk: Telegram-rich-only structures such as tables/details/headings are no longer delivered through `sendRichMessage` on the default final reply path; unsupported rich tags are escaped/degraded through legacy HTML so clients can render the message instead of showing "not supported". - Draft streaming and explicit rich edit paths are left unchanged; this PR targets the default final reply transport that caused the full channel outage. - Rollback: revert this commit to restore the previous rich-message default path. ## Security / privacy No credentials, tokens, private endpoints, account identifiers, or local hostnames are included in the implementation or tests. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: Telegram final reply delivery should use the legacy Bot API `sendMessage` path with `parse_mode: "HTML"` for ordinary replies. It must not call `sendRichMessage` for default final-reply delivery, because #93263 reports standard Telegram clients rendering those rich messages as "not supported". - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, running the Telegram delivery module from this PR branch with Node/tsx. No real Telegram token was used and no external Telegram message was sent; the command captures the Bot API payload produced by the changed delivery code path. - Exact steps or command run after fix: 1. Run the changed Telegram delivery module from this branch. 2. Invoke `sendTelegramText` with Markdown text, `linkPreview: false`, and `replyToMessageId: 7`. 3. Capture which Bot API method the delivery path calls and the params it emits. ```bash corepack pnpm exec tsx /tmp/telegram-proof.mjs ``` - Evidence after fix: Console output from the command above: ```json { "messageId": 42, "calls": [ { "method": "sendMessage", "chatId": "123", "text": "hello https://example.com", "params": { "parse_mode": "HTML", "link_preview_options": { "is_disabled": true }, "reply_to_message_id": 7, "allow_sending_without_reply": true } }, { "log": "telegram sendMessage ok chat=123 message=42" } ] } ``` - Observed result after fix: The after-fix final-reply delivery path emitted exactly one legacy `sendMessage` call with `parse_mode: "HTML"`, preserved disabled link previews and reply params, and did not call `sendRichMessage` for ordinary replies. - What was not tested: A live Telegram network send with a real bot token was not performed from this PR branch to avoid using or exposing credentials in public evidence. Draft streaming and explicit rich edit paths are also not covered by this fix; the PR targets ordinary final-reply delivery, which is the outage path described in #93263.
target.author: snowzlm
